transcript:
Right away you're looking at a ring of blue-white particles orbiting a black hole of nothing — and here's the thing, that dark center isn't a gap in the drawing, it's actively rendered. Look at the void interior: there's a shader making the center *darker than the background itself*, blending from a color called void core out to what the code literally calls depth of absence. The void is being drawn as a thing, not left blank.

Then check the physics loop, where each particle tracks a variable called effort — how hard it's straining against a boundary it can't cross. Near the void's edge there's a comment: "this is where identity happens: what you cannot enter makes you what you are." That effort value doesn't just move particles, it brightens them — the shader mixes their color toward a shade called boundary glow, so the ring's glow is literally a visualization of frustration.

So what you're watching isn't particles circling a hole. It's a boundary constantly proving its own existence through everything that fails to cross it — the void has no substance of its own except what the crowd's yearning defines.
